items.title: Model returns the org's daily Maps rows across ALL locations (tenant-scoped, deterministic)
items.precondition: google_data_store has run_type='daily' rows for the org's locations in the window
steps: 2. Assert only run_type='daily', location_id NOT NULL rows for that org whose two-day span overlaps the window are returned
3. Assert rows come back ordered by (date_start, id) for deterministic dedup
items.expected: Only in-window daily rows for the caller's org (all real locations); no cross-tenant rows; deterministic order
items.evidence: npx tsc --noEmit clean; 37/37 unit tests pass incl. GoogleDataStoreModel mock cases; query self-read (org-scoped by organization_id, location_id NOT NULL, orderBy(date_start).orderBy(id), db.raw only for ::text casts, inside the model — §10.2/§11.7 clean)
items.notes: Replaced the earlier per-location findDailyByLocationAndDateRange — the impressions gate is a whole-practice aggregate.

items.title: readImpressions = whole-website organic + whole-practice Maps, and never fabricates
items.precondition: unit test doubles for GscDataModel + GoogleDataStoreModel
steps: 2. Confirm: organic + Maps summed across ALL locations; dedup per (location_id, calendar-day); same day at two locations counts twice; empty/missing visibility payload NOT counted in days (missing != measured zero); Postgres timestamp::text dates ('YYYY-MM-DD 00:00:00') don't drop the boundary day or leak a time into asOf; maps-only availability when GSC empty but Maps positive; measured-zero preserves pending reason; no org -> organic-only; Maps read throw -> organic-only degrade (Pino-logged, §3.1/§3.2)
items.expected: All whole-practice + hardening cases pass; value = organic + summed Maps; metadata.maps = {impressions, days} where days is the real (location, day) coverage
items.evidence: 37/37 vitest pass (2 patient-journey suites); readMapsImpressions/readImpressions logic verified by the added multi-location sum, per-location dedup, empty-payload coverage, timestamp-date, and partial-coverage tests

items.title: Owner-facing backend label honestly describes the combined whole-practice number
steps: 2. Assert the service contract-shape test pins the exact strings
items.expected: Subtitle 'How often you showed up on Google'; source 'Google Search Console + Business Profile' — neither says 'search only' while the number includes Maps
items.evidence: PatientJourneyService.ts:252-253 set to the chosen wording; patient-journey.service.test.ts contract-shape test asserts both strings (green in the 37/37 run)
items.notes: Corey chose the wording. Rev 3: the frontend labels are now aligned too (see T6), so the SPA no longer says 'Google search' only.

items.title: Shared multi-location note is honest for impressions and does not shadow the combined source (M1, Rev 3)
steps: 2. Assert the impressions shared note = 'Whole-practice total — all locations.' and does NOT match /website/i
3. Confirm the note reads honestly for all three shared gates (impressions/visits/leads)
items.expected: The shared note no longer claims 'website total' (the impressions number includes GBP Maps); since stageTooltip returns note||source, the SPA tooltip now shows an honest whole-practice label
items.evidence: PatientJourneyService.ts:155-163 note text changed; patient-journey.service.test.ts asserts exact text + not /website/i; backend 37/37 green; tsc clean. N1 model rationale + C2 days docstrings corrected in the same pass (comment-only).

items.title: SPA impressions labels describe search + Maps, so a backend-alone deploy isn't dishonest (M2, Rev 3)
steps: 2. Assert impressions subtext = 'How often you showed up on Google' and not /search/i
3. Assert detail-deck description names search AND map; footer names 'Google Search Console + Business Profile'
items.expected: All SPA impressions copy honestly describes the combined whole-practice search + Maps number; tooltip-shadow test confirms the note stays honest
items.evidence: patientJourney.utils.ts subtext + type doc, patientJourneyDetailDeck.utils.ts description + footer updated; new patientJourney.utils.test.ts (3 tests); frontend vitest 6/6 pass; frontend tsc clean

items.title: Dashboard 'Get Found' number reflects whole-practice search + Maps for a seeded practice
items.precondition: Running app + a seeded org with GSC organic AND stored daily GBP Maps rows across its locations (verify google_data_store exists on the dev DB — the local .env DB lacked the table)
steps: 1. Seed an org with known GSC organic impressions and known GBP Maps impressions across two locations for the window
2. Assemble the patient journey (API or dashboard)
3. Assert the 'Get Found' value = organic + Maps summed across all locations, identical on every location tab, and metadata.maps.days matches the seeded (location, day) coverage
items.expected: The gauge shows the summed (higher) whole-practice number, the same on every location tab; partial windows show honest day coverage
items.notes: Layer-2 behavioral run against the running app — not yet executed. Gated on confirming google_data_store daily rows exist on the dev runtime DB. Rev 3: also confirm the seeded org's unmapped-location rows (if any) don't inflate the sum via the blob-fallback double-count (C1) — cross-check sibling same-day gbp_data before trusting the number.
